# readme.md

Template for the Nano/TypeScript tier scaffold.

## Generated File: `README.md`

```markdown
# {{PROJECT_NAME}}

{{PROJECT_DESCRIPTION}}

Built on the Nano/TypeScript scaffold -- a lightweight, modular personal assistant framework.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Node.js >= 20
- npm (or pnpm)

### 1. Install dependencies

npm install

### 2. Configure environment

Copy the example env file and fill in your values:

cp .env.example .env

Required variables:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `{{PROVIDER_API_KEY_ENV}}` | API key for the {{PROVIDER_TYPE_A}} provider |
| `PROVIDER_TYPE` | LLM provider to use (`{{PROVIDER_TYPE_A}}` or `{{PROVIDER_TYPE_B}}`) |
| `CHANNEL_TYPE` | Input channel (`{{CHANNEL_TYPE_A}}` or `{{CHANNEL_TYPE_B}}`) |

Optional variables:

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `PROJECT_NAME` | `{{PROJECT_NAME}}` | Display name in logs |
| `PORT` | `{{DEFAULT_PORT}}` | HTTP server port |
| `PROVIDER_MODEL` | `{{DEFAULT_MODEL}}` | Model identifier |
| `PROVIDER_MAX_TOKENS` | `{{DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS}}` | Max response tokens |
| `PROVIDER_TEMPERATURE` | `{{DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE}}` | Sampling temperature |
| `PROVIDER_BASE_URL` | _(none)_ | Custom provider API base URL |
| `WEBHOOK_PATH` | `{{DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_PATH}}` | Webhook endpoint path |
| `CHANNEL_SECRET` | _(none)_ | Webhook verification secret |
| `SYSTEM_PROMPT` | _(built-in)_ | Custom system prompt |

### 3. Run

Development (with auto-reload):

npm run dev

Production:

npm run build
npm start

## Docker

Build and run with Docker:

docker build -t {{PROJECT_NAME}} .
docker run -d --name {{PROJECT_NAME}} --env-file .env -p {{DEFAULT_PORT}}:{{DEFAULT_PORT}} {{PROJECT_NAME}}

## Project Structure

{{PROJECT_NAME}}/
  src/
    index.ts        # Entry point -- wires config, provider, and channel
    config.ts       # Environment loading and validation
    provider.ts     # LLM provider abstraction
    channel.ts      # Input channel abstraction (webhook, bot, etc.)
  dist/             # Compiled JavaScript (generated by `npm run build`)
  Dockerfile
  tsconfig.json
  package.json
  .env.example

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run dev` | Start in development mode with watch/reload |
| `npm run build` | Compile TypeScript to `dist/` |
| `npm start` | Run the compiled application |
| `npm run typecheck` | Run type checking without emitting |
| `npm run clean` | Remove the `dist/` directory |

## Architecture

The application follows a three-layer design:

1. **Config** (`config.ts`) -- Loads and validates environment variables into typed configuration objects.
2. **Provider** (`provider.ts`) -- Wraps the LLM API. Swappable between {{PROVIDER_TYPE_A}} and {{PROVIDER_TYPE_B}}.
3. **Channel** (`channel.ts`) -- Handles incoming messages and outgoing replies. Swappable between {{CHANNEL_TYPE_A}} and {{CHANNEL_TYPE_B}}.

The entry point (`index.ts`) wires these together and runs the message loop.

## License

{{LICENSE}}
```

## Placeholders

| Placeholder | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| `{{PROJECT_NAME}}` | Project display name and Docker image name |
| `{{PROJECT_DESCRIPTION}}` | One-line project description |
| `{{PROVIDER_API_KEY_ENV}}` | Name of the API key env var (e.g. `OPENAI_API_KEY`) |
| `{{PROVIDER_TYPE_A}}` | Primary provider identifier |
| `{{PROVIDER_TYPE_B}}` | Secondary provider identifier |
| `{{CHANNEL_TYPE_A}}` | Primary channel identifier |
| `{{CHANNEL_TYPE_B}}` | Secondary channel identifier |
| `{{DEFAULT_PORT}}` | Default port number |
| `{{DEFAULT_MODEL}}` | Default model identifier |
| `{{DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS}}` | Default max tokens |
| `{{DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE}}` | Default temperature |
| `{{DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_PATH}}` | Default webhook path |
| `{{LICENSE}}` | License identifier (e.g. `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`) |
